peaks.name: Mount Rainier

permits.name: Mount Rainier Climbing Permit
permits.description: Required for all climbers traveling above 10,000 ft or on glaciers. Mandatory year-round.
permits.duration: Valid for permit date + 1 calendar day
permits.notes: 2/3 of permits available for advance reservation May-September; 1/3 first-come, first-served at ranger stations

permits.name: Mount Rainier Wilderness Permit
permits.description: Required for all overnight backcountry stays

where_to_obtain.lottery: Early-access lottery in February; general reservations April 25 for June 1-Oct 10

permits.notes: Peak season (June-Sept) highly competitive. Walk-up permits available at ranger stations same day or day before

parking_passes.type: Mount Rainier National Park Vehicle Pass
parking_passes.covers: Vehicle entrance to Mount Rainier National Park for 7 consecutive days
parking_passes.where_to_buy: Entrance gates, online at recreation.gov, major retail locations
parking_passes.notes: Covers vehicle and all passengers. Motorcycle $25/day or $50/year

parking_passes.covers: Entrance to all federal recreation sites including Mount Rainier NP
parking_passes.notes: Valid 12 months from purchase. Digital option available since January 2026

seasonal.peak_season_notes: Climbing permits fill quickly in summer
seasonal.spring_recommended: Late June recommended to avoid avalanche danger

road_closures.notes: Closes due to snow; check NPS website for current status

road_closures.location: SR-410 Chinook Pass and SR-123 Cayuse Pass
road_closures.notes: Subject to weather; 2026 construction 2.8 miles April-October with 30 min delays possible

group_limits.notes: Large groups may require special coordination

climbing_specific.avalanche_forecasting: NWAC forecasts available; some routes may close
climbing_specific.technical_requirements: Rope skills, glacier travel proficiency, excellent physical condition

peaks.notes: Mount Rainier is the most heavily regulated peak in Washington. Permits fill quickly in summer. The park uses proven traffic and parking management strategies. No timed entry reservations required in 2026.

peaks.name: Mount Adams

land_manager.district_hours: Monday, Wednesday, Friday 9am-4pm (closed 12-1pm lunch); Closed Tue-Thu, Sat-Sun

permits.name: Mt. Adams Climbing Activity Pass
permits.description: Required for anyone 16+ traveling above 7,000 ft elevation May 1-Sept 30
permits.duration: Valid for single climbing trip only
permits.notes: Pass is required even if not attempting summit; applies to any travel above 7,000 ft

permits.name: Gifford Pinchot Wilderness Permit
permits.description: Required to enter Mount Adams Wilderness; self-issued

parking_passes.covers: Day-use parking at National Forest recreation sites
parking_passes.where_to_buy: Recreation.gov, ranger stations, select retail locations

seasonal.spring_access: Limited; snow typically remains until June
seasonal.winter_access: Not recommended; severe avalanche and weather hazards

special_closures.status: Closed at milepost 45.2 due to large slide
special_closures.notes: Check current road conditions before trip

climbing_specific.hazard_waste_requirement: Pack out human waste in carry-out bags

camping.sanitation: Pack out human waste in carry-out bags

peaks.notes: Mount Adams Climbing Pass required May 1-Sept 30 for ages 16+. Free wilderness permits self-issued at trailheads. Popular summer destination with good climbing window.

peaks.name: Mount Baker

permits.name: Mount Baker Climbing
permits.description: No specific climbing permit required by USFS
permits.notes: USFS does not require permits; however, recreation passes required at trailheads

permits.name: Wilderness Permit (if in protected area)
permits.description: May be required depending on access route and destination

parking_passes.where_to_buy: Must purchase online or download Recreation.gov Scan and Pay app; NOT available onsite
parking_passes.notes: Passes not available at trailhead kiosks

group_limits.notes: Check with Mt. Baker Ranger District for group size recommendations

climbing_specific.route_complexity: Multiple route options ranging from moderate to technical

peaks.notes: Mount Baker lies primarily on USFS land (Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie NF). Main issue: Recreation passes required at popular trailheads and must be purchased in advance (online) or via mobile app - NOT available at trailhead.

peaks.name: Mount Shuksan

permits.name: North Cascades Backcountry Permit
permits.description: Required for overnight backcountry stays; free for day-use

where_to_obtain.alt_method: In-person at Glacier Public Service Center (Thurs-Fri June 25-Sept 25)
where_to_obtain.alt_method_2: Email noca_wilderness@nps.gov no more than 3 days before trip

permits.seasonal_note: Wilderness Information Center open mid-May to mid-October

parking_passes.type: North Cascades National Park Vehicle Pass
parking_passes.covers: Vehicle entrance to North Cascades NP for 7 days

seasonal.winter_access: Severe hazard; technical winter climbing only

road_closures.reason: Washout damage from December 2025 storm; emergency repairs completed June 2026

group_limits.notes: Check specific site limits when reserving

climbing_specific.crevasse_rescue_equipment: Required for glacier and technical terrain
climbing_specific.technical_requirements: Alpine climbing experience; rope skills recommended

camping.bear_canister_requirement: Required at certain camps; must borrow in-person

peaks.notes: Mount Shuksan located in North Cascades NP. Lake Ann Trailhead (popular access) requires Recreation Pass. Free day-use permits; overnight permits $10/person + $6 reservation fee. Popular area; midweek or post-Labor Day visits less crowded.

peaks.name: Mount Stuart

land_manager.district_hours: Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri 9am-12pm and 1-3:30pm (closed 12-1pm lunch, closed Wed)

permits.name: Free Self-Issue Day Permit
permits.description: Required for day-use; free self-issuing permit

permits.name: Enchantment Overnight Permit
permits.description: Required May 15-Oct 31 for overnight camping in Enchantments/Alpine Lakes

where_to_obtain.lottery_type: Advanced lottery (apply in spring for summer dates)

seasonal.spring_access: May 15 open date for overnight permits

group_limits.dog_restrictions: Dogs prohibited on Colchuck Lake trail, Stuart Lake trail, and within Enchantment Permit Area
group_limits.notes: Group limit strictly enforced in permit area

climbing_specific.crevasse_rescue_equipment: Recommended for glacier and steep terrain
climbing_specific.technical_requirements: Class 3-4 scrambling; rock climbing skills recommended

peaks.notes: Mount Stuart accessed via Alpine Lakes Wilderness, often through Enchantments. Overnight permits highly competitive (lottery); demand far exceeds availability. Advanced reservations strongly recommended. Dogs prohibited in Enchantments.

peaks.name: Glacier Peak

land_manager.unit: Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest (primary) / North Cascades National Park (partial)

permits.name: Self-Issue Wilderness Permit
permits.description: Free self-issuing permit required for all wilderness use

where_to_obtain.alt_method: May be available at ranger stations

permits.notes: Some trailheads may require recreation pass; always available at trailhead

seasonal.spring_access: Limited by snow; June possible on lower elevation routes
seasonal.trail_access_status: As of April 2026, access extremely limited due to December 2025 storm damage; check current conditions

climbing_specific.technical_requirements: Alpine climbing skills; glacier travel proficiency

peaks.notes: Glacier Peak is centerpiece of 566,057-acre wilderness. Permits are free and self-issued. CRITICAL: Trail access severely limited as of April 2026 due to December 2025 atmospheric river damage. Check current conditions with Wenatchee River Ranger District before trip planning.

peaks.name: Dragontail Peak

permits.name: Enchantment Overnight Permit
permits.description: Dragontail Peak accessed via Colchuck Lake in Enchantments; overnight permits required May 15-Oct 31
permits.notes: Demand far exceeds availability; advanced reservations essential

seasonal.notes: Dragontail Peak rises 3,000 ft above south end of Colchuck Lake

group_limits.dog_restrictions: Dogs prohibited on Colchuck Lake trail and in Enchantment Permit Area

climbing_specific.technical_requirements: Class 3-4 scrambling; experienced alpine skills needed

peaks.notes: Dragontail is accessed from Enchantments area (Stuart Lake access). Rising 3,000 feet above Colchuck Lake, it's a notable scrambling peak. Lottery system highly competitive. Contact Wenatchee River Ranger District at 509-548-2550 for permit availability and lottery dates.

peaks.name: Mount Triumph

parking_passes.type: North Cascades National Park Vehicle Pass

climbing_specific.crevasse_rescue_equipment: May be needed depending on route

peaks.notes: Mount Triumph is located within North Cascades National Park. Requires backcountry permits for overnight stays ($10/person + $6 fee). Day-use free permits available. Contact park at 360-854-7200 for detailed access and route information.

peaks.name: Mount Formidable

permits.name: North Cascades Backcountry Permit
permits.description: Required for overnight backcountry use in park

where_to_obtain.method: Recreation.gov or in-person at Glacier Public Service Center (Thurs-Fri June 25-Sept 25)

parking_passes.type: North Cascades National Park Vehicle Pass

climbing_specific.technical_requirements: Class 4 climbing; significant alpine climbing experience required

peaks.notes: Mount Formidable within North Cascades National Park. Overnight backcountry permits required ($10/person + $6 reservation fee). Permits available through Recreation.gov. Contact park at 360-854-7200 for specific route and access information.

peaks.name: Liberty Bell Group

permits.name: North Cascades Wilderness Permit (if applicable)
permits.description: May be required depending on specific access route

seasonal.road_status: SR-20 North Cascades Highway: closed Dec 4, 2025; reopened June 14, 2026

climbing_specific.technical_requirements: Class 3-4 rock climbing; excellent scrambling skills required

peaks.notes: Liberty Bell Group (Liberty Bell Mountain and Early Winters Spires) located near Washington Pass area. Washington Pass Observation Site provides views of Liberty Bell. Permits not typically required for day-use scrambling, but Northwest Forest Pass needed at trailheads. Access via SR-20 which fully reopened June 14, 2026 after storm damage repairs.

peaks.name: Mount Lago

camping.campfire_restrictions: Check current restrictions with ranger district

peaks.notes: Mount Lago located within Pasayten Wilderness, Okanogan-Wenatchee NF. Free wilderness permits self-issued at trailhead. Group size limit 12 persons maximum. Contact Methow Valley Ranger District at 509-996-4000 for access and trail conditions.

peaks.name: Robinson Mountain
peaks.notes: Robinson Mountain in Pasayten Wilderness. Free self-issued permits. Major stock route into Remmel-Cathedral area. Access via Robinson Creek-Middle Fork Trail. Contact Methow Valley Ranger District for trail conditions and access details.

peaks.name: Remmel Mountain
peaks.notes: Remmel Mountain in Pasayten Wilderness. Free permits self-issued at trailhead. Part of Remmel-Cathedral area accessible via major stock route from Robinson Creek area. Contact Methow Valley Ranger District for current trail and access conditions.

peaks.name: Ruby Mountain

land_manager.agency: National Park Service / U.S. Forest Service
land_manager.unit: North Cascades National Park or adjacent wilderness

permits.name: North Cascades Backcountry Permit (if within park)
permits.notes: Permit requirements depend on specific access route and location within North Cascades complex

parking_passes.type: North Cascades National Park Vehicle Pass or Northwest Forest Pass

peaks.notes: Ruby Mountain in North Cascades area. Exact permit requirements depend on specific access route. Contact North Cascades National Park at 360-854-7200 for detailed permit and access information.

peaks.name: Elephant Butte

land_manager.agency: National Park Service / U.S. Forest Service
land_manager.unit: North Cascades National Park or adjacent wilderness

permits.name: North Cascades Backcountry Permit (if within park)

parking_passes.type: North Cascades National Park Vehicle Pass or Northwest Forest Pass

peaks.notes: Elephant Butte in North Cascades area. Permit requirements depend on specific access. Contact North Cascades National Park at 360-854-7200 for access details and permit information.

peaks.name: Castle Peak

parking_passes.type: Mount Rainier National Park Vehicle Pass

peaks.notes: Castle Peak located in Tatoosh Range within Mount Rainier National Park. Overnight permits required ($12/person/night + $6 fee). Day-use is free. Contact Mount Rainier NP at 360-569-2211 for specific access and trail information.

peaks.name: Indian Head Peak

land_manager.unit: Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest (primary) / North Cascades National Park (partial)

seasonal.special_note: Trail access may be limited due to 2025 storm damage; check current conditions

peaks.notes: Indian Head Peak located in Glacier Peak Wilderness. Free self-issued permits at trailhead. Trail access severely limited as of April 2026 due to December 2025 atmospheric river damage. Contact Wenatchee River Ranger District at 509-548-2550 for current conditions before trip.

peaks.name: Big Snagtooth

seasonal.road_status: SR-20 North Cascades Highway: closed Dec 4, 2025; reopened June 14, 2026

peaks.notes: Big Snagtooth located in Pasayten Wilderness area near Washington Pass. Free self-issued permits at trailhead. Access via SR-20 which fully reopened June 14, 2026 after storm repairs. Contact Methow Valley Ranger District for current trail conditions.

peaks.name: Amphitheater Mountain
peaks.notes: Amphitheater Mountain in Pasayten Wilderness. Free wilderness permits self-issued at trailhead. Group size limit 12 persons maximum. Contact Methow Valley Ranger District at 509-996-4000 for access information and trail conditions.

summary.research_completeness: Comprehensive for priority peaks; moderate detail for secondary peaks. All major permit systems, parking passes, contact information, and seasonal access documented.

nps_peaks: Mount Rainier NP: $82/person climbing permit + $12/person/night wilderness camping
North Cascades NP: $10/person + $6 fee for backcountry permits (covers Shuksan, Triumph, Formidable)
usfs_peaks: Gifford Pinchot (Mount Adams): $20 Mount Adams Climbing Pass (ages 16+) + free wilderness permit
Okanogan-Wenatchee (Stuart, Glacier Peak, etc.): Alpine Lakes Enchantments $5/person/day + $6 lottery fee; Pasayten/Glacier Peak free self-issued permits
Mount Baker-Snoqualmie: No climbing permit required, but Northwest Forest Pass ($5/day or $30/annual) required at trailheads
parking_passes_required: Northwest Forest Pass ($5/day, $30/annual): Required at USFS trailheads
America the Beautiful Pass ($80/annual): Covers all federal sites
Mount Rainier NP vehicle pass ($30/day, $55/annual): Mount Rainier access
North Cascades NP vehicle pass ($30/day): North Cascades access
seasonal_closures: SR-20 North Cascades Highway: Closed Dec 4, 2025; Reopened June 14, 2026 (post-storm repairs)
Mount Rainier passes: Typically close Nov 1; reopen late May (Chinook Pass reopened May 22, 2026)
Alpine Lakes Enchantments: Permits May 15 - Oct 31 only (lottery-based)
special_alerts: December 2025 atmospheric river caused significant trail damage in Glacier Peak and surrounding areas; access limited as of April 2026
Upper SR-504 near Mount Adams closed due to large slide; check current status
Mount Rainier no timed-entry reservations required in 2026

sources_used: fs.usda.gov (Gifford Pinchot, Okanogan-Wenatchee, Mount Baker-Snoqualmie National Forests)

Implement ranger district contact info display on each peak detail view
Add seasonal access window widget showing typical open/close dates
Link parking pass requirements to pass provider websites (recreation.gov)
Set up alert system for road closures and trail access updates from WSDOT/NWAC
Consider data refresh schedule for 2027 permit costs and contact info (likely annual update)

data_quality_notes.high_confidence: All 10 priority peaks have complete permit, parking, and contact information
data_quality_notes.moderate_confidence: Secondary peaks have general permit info; specific trailhead details may vary
data_quality_notes.research_date_note: Data reflects 2026 regulations and fees current as of July 15, 2026
